data.title: Jiangsu Securities Bureau Warns Against Hong Kong 'Pseudo-Exchanges'
data.description: The Jiangsu Securities Regulatory Bureau has issued a risk warning regarding 'pseudo-exchanges' in Hong Kong that offer listing and bell-ringing services in exchange for payment. According to NS3.AI, the bureau highlighted that certain intermediaries are providing fake stock codes, website promotions, and fabricated ceremonies. These activities are being used by some companies to market equity and 'original shares' to the public, raising concerns about illegal fundraising practices.

data.title: Major talks between Iran and the United States will be held tomorrow.
data.description: Pakistani media confirmed that an Iranian delegation has arrived for negotiations. Major talks between Iran and the United States will be held tomorrow, focusing on reopening the Strait of Hormuz and extending the ceasefire. (Jinshi)

data.title: The European Central Bank supports EU regulatory reforms, and crypto asset servicers will be subject to unified regulation by the ESMA.
data.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) has expressed support for the European Commission's plan to centralize financial regulation. This plan aims to consolidate the oversight of cross-border financial institutions, such as crypto asset service providers (CASPs), from national regulators to the European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA). This move seeks to deepen the integration of the EU's capital markets and enhance its international competitiveness. The ECB emphasized that the reform requires providing the ESMA with sufficient staff and funding, and minimizing market disruption during the regulatory transition period. (Reuters)

data.title: Circle responds to Drift theft: Calls for expedited crypto regulatory legislation; USDC freeze must be enforced in accordance with the law.
data.description: Circle's Chief Strategy Officer, Dante Disparte, responded to the Drift Protocol's theft of over $270 million on April 1st. He stated that Circle's freezing of USDC was only carried out when legally mandated, not a unilateral decision. This action was neither a backdoor nor algorithmic monitoring, but rather a manifestation of the rule of law in internet-native finance. He pointed out that the core contradiction of open systems is that the legal framework lags behind technological development. Protocols, wallets, exchanges, and stablecoin issuers need to consider security and accountability as shared responsibilities. DeFi protocols can learn from traditional financial circuit breaker mechanisms to build on-chain protection. He also called for the US's GENIUS Act and CLARITY Act to be enacted before the next major security incident, incorporating due process, property rights, and financial privacy protection standards into law.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Daly: If the US-Iran ceasefire continues, CPI data will be irrelevant.
data.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Canada President Tom Daly stated that the real question is whether the ceasefire can be sustained; if it can, then the CPI data will be irrelevant. (Jinshi)

data.title: Fed's Daly: Ceasefire's Longevity More Crucial Than CPI Data
data.description: Federal Reserve official Mary Daly emphasized that the primary concern is whether a ceasefire can be sustained. According to Jin10, Daly noted that if the ceasefire holds, the Consumer Price Index (CPI) data becomes less significant. This perspective highlights the importance of geopolitical stability over economic indicators in current assessments.

data.title: Bloomberg analysts: Bitcoin "day/night" ETFs launched on the same day; NGHT rebounded, but its future performance remains to be seen.
data.description: Eric Balchunas, senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg, stated on the X platform that while the market is focused on the listing of the Morgan Stanley Bitcoin ETF (MSBT), another Bitcoin exchange-traded fund (ETF), the Bitcoin After Dark ETF (NGHT), also launched this Wednesday. The NGHT's first-day trading performance was lackluster, but trading volume increased significantly on the second day, and its future performance remains to be seen. For long-term ETF holders, holding products like MSBT can already yield good returns. However, if NGHT experiences strong subsequent inflows, it may gain market acceptance; otherwise, it could quickly become marginalized. The Morgan Stanley Bitcoin ETF, with its advantageous distribution channels, is expected to achieve significant success.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin encountered resistance after reaching $72,000; CPI and US-Iran negotiations become key variables.
data.description: Bitcoin has risen nearly 7% since Sunday, but its gains slowed after reaching the $72,000 range, with institutional funds showing unclear direction. The market is focused on two major uncertainties: the US March CPI data (expected to exceed 3% year-on-year, driven by energy prices) and the progress of weekend US-Iran negotiations. The options market shows that institutions are simultaneously favoring $80,000 call options on Deribit and related call options on BlackRock IBIT, while maintaining a high proportion of put options to hedge downside risk. If the CPI exceeds expectations, it could strengthen expectations of a Fed rate hike, putting pressure on risk assets, including Bitcoin. (CoinDesk)

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | ANZ and Goldman Sachs Maintain Bullish Gold Forecasts Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty
data.description:
ANZ has maintained its year-end gold target at $5,800, citing central bank demand, geopolitical uncertainty, anticipated Federal Reserve rate cuts, and diversification away from US dollar assets as factors supporting a long-term bullish outlook. According to NS3.AI, these elements contribute to a positive sentiment for gold prices in the future.

Goldman Sachs also upheld its forecast of $5,400, noting that prolonged disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz could bolster gold prices over the long term, despite potential short-term downside risks. Both financial institutions emphasize the impact of geopolitical factors and monetary policy on the precious metal's market trajectory.

data.title: Commodities to Outshine Stocks Amid Geopolitical and Economic Turmoil, Says Bank of America Strategist
data.description:
Bank of America strategist Michael Hartnett has indicated that investors should pivot towards the commodities market in the coming years, as this asset class is poised to benefit from global geopolitical and macroeconomic instability. According to Jin10, the ongoing Middle East conflicts and the race in artificial intelligence have heightened concerns over supply chains. Governments worldwide are striving to curb the soaring prices of energy and other natural resources, which impact industries and consumers, while also ensuring the supply of critical minerals like rare earths, essential for manufacturing and technology.

Hartnett suggests that for the remainder of this decade, commodities will replace stocks as the primary choice for investors seeking to hedge against risks, inflation, and a weakening dollar. This shift is expected as investors move away from equities in favor of commodities, which are anticipated to become the biggest winners in the "buy anything but bonds" trade. The strategist also notes that excessive fiscal expansion could lead to more frequent bear market rallies in government bonds rather than sustained bull markets.

data.title: RWA tokenization platform Libera has completed a new round of financing, with AlloyX Ventures participating.
data.description: Libeara, a real-world asset (RWA) tokenization platform, announced the completion of a new round of financing. AlloyX Ventures, the venture capital arm of Nasdaq-listed SOLOWIN HOLDINGS, participated in the investment. The specific investment amount was not disclosed. The new funds will be used to advance the construction of compliant digital infrastructure. (Globenewswire)

data.title: Dingdian Financial Technology plans to launch a regulated Hong Kong dollar stablecoin, HKDAP, in phases during Q2.
data.description: Animoca Brands announced on its X platform that Dingdian Fintech, a joint venture established by Standard Chartered Bank, HKT and Animoca Brands, will begin phased development of the regulated Hong Kong dollar-backed stablecoin HKDAP (HKD At Par) starting in the second quarter of this year. Group President Evan Auyang stated that a properly governed Hong Kong dollar-backed stablecoin is crucial to Hong Kong's financial infrastructure.

data.title: Anthropic and CoreWeave reach data center leasing agreement
data.description: Anthropic has agreed to lease data center capacity from CoreWeave to address the growing demand for its AI services. CoreWeave said in a statement Friday that the multi-year agreement will help Anthropic build and deploy its Claude AI model. CoreWeave CEO Anthony Interrato said the agreement will include multiple Nvidia chip architectures within U.S. data centers. The two companies declined to disclose the financial terms of the agreement. Like OpenAI, Anthropic has been at the forefront of the AI ​​services boom, sometimes facing what it calls “unprecedented demand” that has made it difficult to keep its products online. Earlier this week, Anthropic announced partnerships with Broadcom and Google to acquire 3.5 gigawatts of energy (1 gigawatt of electricity is enough to power approximately 750,000 U.S. homes simultaneously). (Jinshi)

data.title: Arab media: Lebanon-Israel talks will precede US-Iran talks.
data.description: According to the Arab media outlet Asharq News, negotiations between Lebanon and Israel will precede those between Iran and the United States. A preparatory teleconference was held today, with participants including Israeli Ambassador to Washington Nada Hamad Moawad, Israeli Ambassador to Washington Yeshir Light, US Ambassador to Lebanon Michelle Issa, and US State Department Director of Policy Planning Mike Needham. A face-to-face meeting between Lebanon and Israel is scheduled for Tuesday, chaired by Lebanese Ambassador Hamad Moawad. The Lebanese talks are now being overseen by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio and are completely independent of the Iran and Islamabad talks. (Jinshi)

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Secures CoreWeave Data Center Capacity Amid Rising AI Demand
data.description: Anthropic has agreed to lease data center capacity from CoreWeave to address the growing demand for its AI services, Jin10 reports. CoreWeave announced on Friday that the multi-year agreement will assist Anthropic in building and deploying its Claude AI model. CoreWeave's CEO, Intrator, stated that the deal will involve various Nvidia chip architectures within U.S. data centers. The financial terms of the agreement were not disclosed by either company. Anthropic, similar to OpenAI, has been at the forefront of the AI service surge, sometimes struggling to maintain its products online due to what it describes as 'unprecedented demand.' Earlier this week, Anthropic revealed a collaboration with Broadcom and Google to secure 3.5 gigawatts of energy, noting that one gigawatt of power is sufficient to supply electricity to approximately 750,000 U.S. households simultaneously.

data.title: U.S. Government Pushes CLARITY Act to Reshape Cryptocurrency Oversight
data.description: The White House, along with the Treasury, SEC, and CFTC, is intensifying efforts to advance the CLARITY Act in the Senate, aiming to transform the regulatory framework of the $2.4 trillion cryptocurrency market. According to NS3.AI, the White House Council of Economic Advisers has indicated that prohibiting stablecoin yields would only marginally increase U.S. bank lending by $2.1 billion within a $12 trillion market. Additionally, a proposal from FinCEN and OFAC dated April 8 suggests categorizing U.S. stablecoin issuers as financial institutions under the Bank Secrecy Act. SEC Chair Paul Atkins and CFTC Chair Mike Selig have expressed readiness to enforce the bill, contingent upon congressional action.

data.title: Chief Executive of the Hong Kong Monetary Authority: Stablecoin purchases or uses should be made through regulated channels.
data.description: Eddie Yue, Chief Executive of the Hong Kong Monetary Authority (HKMA), stated that the first batch of stablecoin issuers were granted licenses today. According to the licensees' business plans, they intend to commence operations in the coming months after completing relevant preparatory work. Yue expressed his expectation that issuers will proceed with their operations as planned, actively expanding their business scope while effectively managing risks, promoting the application of compliant stablecoins, and addressing pain points in financial and economic activities. He also urged the public to be vigilant against any fraudulent activities or scams claiming to be related to licensees or their issued stablecoins, and to use regulated channels when purchasing or using stablecoins. In case of doubt, the public should check the HKMA's records to verify the licensee's identity or contact the licensee for further enquiries.

data.title: Coinbase launches COINSOV, an index for storing the value of Bitcoin and gold.
data.description: Coinbase Asset Management announced a partnership with global index provider MarketVector Indexes to launch the Coinbase Store of Value Index (COINSOV), a value store index designed to integrate digital and traditional assets into a rules-based index framework. The index dynamically adjusts the weighting of Bitcoin and gold based on volatility and rebalances quarterly. While capturing Bitcoin's upside potential, it aims to maintain drawdown levels similar to gold, providing institutional investors with a value store tool that balances digital and traditional assets. (Businesswire)

data.title: China AI Industry Development Alliance Conference: Token Consumption Surges, Intelligent Agent Frameworks Represented by OpenClaw Accelerate Their Rise
data.description: The 17th Plenary Session of the China Artificial Intelligence Industry Development Alliance was held at the National Cybersecurity Base in Dongxihu District, Wuhan. Yu Xiaohui pointed out that artificial intelligence is rapidly entering the era of native intelligence, with four major trends worth noting: First, the capabilities of large-scale foundational models continue to break through, and programming capabilities are rapidly moving from "code completion" to "full-stack development of intelligent agents"; second, intelligent agent frameworks, represented by OpenClaw, are rapidly emerging, and the intelligent agent application ecosystem is experiencing explosive growth; third, the large-scale application of intelligent agents continues to activate the intelligent economy, and the rise in token consumption is driving rapid growth in cloud services; fourth, the security risks such as data privacy and abuse of permissions brought about by the rapid popularization of intelligent agents cannot be ignored, and security governance must be promoted in tandem with application promotion. He stated that the alliance will continue to focus on five aspects: technological innovation, application-driven development, ecosystem building, international cooperation, and security governance, to jointly create a new pattern for the development of the artificial intelligence industry. (China Academy of Information and Communications Technology)

data.title: Caixin: Polymarket's use of USDC for settlement and delivery poses a significant legal risk to participants within China.
data.description: Caixin.com published an article titled "Financial Innovation or Insider Trading: The Rise and Controversy of Polymarkets," which points out that when insider information can be openly monetized, the boundaries of prediction markets have become blurred. This raises the question of whether it's "gambling" disguised as finance, or even insider trading. Regardless of the outcome of the legal debate over whether it's gambling or not, the fact that Polymarkets uses the USDC stablecoin for settlement and delivery poses a significant legal risk to participants within China. Previously, US Senators Merkley and Klobuchar introduced the "End Prediction Market Corruption Act," which would prohibit the president, vice president, and members of Congress from trading in prediction markets and require their spouses and dependent children to include prediction market trading activities in their annual financial disclosures.
